assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Atomic recreation: All slices are recreated entirely in a single step (not rolling)' and emphasizes this is different from regular GPU node maintenance

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'a maintenance event on ANY slice in your multislice environment will trigger restart of ALL 4 slices simultaneously' and explains cascading restart behavior

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All training pods restart simultaneously — there's no graceful migration' and describes the complete interruption of the training job

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response warns 'If insufficient TPU v5e resources exist during recreation, slices will block until hardware becomes available' and mentions 'Could extend downtime from hours to days' in risk mitigation section

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Save training checkpoint immediately' as the first item in the preparation checklist with specific action items

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes verification of 'graceful restart capability' and 'proper checkpoint loading on startup' in the training job configuration section

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer the maintenance event - this option is not discussed anywhere in the response

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: While the response mentions 'maintenance window' in context of preparation, it does not explain that users can control or schedule when the maintenance occurs during planned breaks
